$note: R2 steps 6-7 — semantic-tier eval (embedding fallback). PRECONDITION: every row MUST return [] from the deterministic cascade (label sanity, offline). Embedding part needs OPENAI_API_KEY; sweeps cosine accept-threshold 0.60-0.90, picks smallest accept with precision>=0.90 overall AND per-language, then max recall. Gate: pnpm eval:semantic.

protocol:
HARNESS = a NEW offline-by-default eval (src/calibration/eval-semantic.ts), SEPARATE from eval-mentions (which stays DB/key-free and offline-deterministic). Data file data/eval-semantic.json holds the rows below (mention, lang, expected[], kind, note).

PRECONDITION GATE (label sanity, runs always, no key/DB needed): for every row, assert SkillNormalizerService.normalizeMention(mention) returns [] (no canonical). A row the deterministic cascade already resolves is a LABELING ERROR and fails the run before any embedding work. All 52 rows here were verified to return [] against the live normalizer + skills-pilot.json (one earlier candidate, 'data cleaning and wrangling', resolved via 'and'-split to data_preprocessing and was REMOVED).

OFFLINE NO-OP CONTRACT: when NODE_ENV=test OR no OPENAI_API_KEY OR no DB, the embedding tier must no-op cleanly (resolve nothing, throw nothing). In that mode eval-semantic runs ONLY the precondition gate + prints 'SKIPPED (no embeddings)' for the scoring section and exits 0. The 0.70-0.85 sweep below runs only when a key + skill-embedding matrix are present (CI job with the secret, or local).

EMBED ONCE: build the skill-embedding matrix exactly as production does — text-embedding-3-large, dimensions=1024 via the OpenAI 'dimensions' param, L2 re-normalize AFTER truncation (fix the known OpenAiProvider.embed() bug that ignores LlmEmbedOptions.dimensions first, else the matrix is 3072-d and every cosine is wrong). One vector per skill TEXT = canonical_name + display_name + each alias, tagged to its canonical (so a hit counts if top-1 maps to any expected canonical). Embed all 52 eval mentions once too. Cache mention->vector and the skill matrix to disk so re-runs cost 0 (mirrors the production phrase->canonical cache).

SCORING: for each mention compute cosine vs every skill vector; top1 = max, top1Canonical = its skill's canonical. A semantic_hit is CORRECT iff top1>=accept AND top1Canonical ∈ expected[] (set membership, primary OR listed alternative both pass). A semantic_negative is CORRECT iff top1<accept (i.e. it is NOT auto-accepted). A hit whose top1>=accept but maps to a canonical NOT in expected[] is a FALSE POSITIVE (wrong-target) and counts against precision — this is why near-domain rows (print design, warehouse, CRM, printer) are in the set.

THRESHOLD SWEEP: per the blueprint, sweep accept-threshold t from 0.60 to 0.90 step 0.01. The task's 0.70-0.85 window is the REPORTING focus (print the full PR curve over that range); compute over 0.60-0.90 so the precision>=0.90 crossing point is visible even if it sits at an edge. At each t: TP=hits correctly accepted to a correct canonical; FP=(hits accepted to wrong canonical)+(negatives accepted); FN=(hits not accepted)+(hits accepted to wrong canonical, also counted as a miss of the right one). precision=TP/(TP+FP), recall=TP/(TP+FN), F1=2PR/(P+R). Emit the precision/recall/F1 triple at every t plus a rendered PR curve over 0.70-0.85.

PER-LANGUAGE: recompute the same sweep on the en subset and the vi subset independently (mixed has 0 rows here) and report both curves; the VI subset is the harder one (translation-distance inflates negatives' cosine), so its precision crossing is the binding constraint.

THRESHOLD PICK: choose accept = the SMALLEST t in [0.60,0.90] where OVERALL precision>=0.90 AND per-language precision>=0.90 for BOTH en and vi, then among those pick the t with MAX overall recall (favor precision per blueprint; ties -> lower t for recall). needs_review band = [accept-0.08, accept); below accept-0.08 = none. Record the chosen accept + the implied review-band edges in the run output and write them back to the blueprint (do NOT hardcode 0.78 — the blueprint value is a placeholder to be replaced by this run's result).

PASS BARS for SHIPPING the tier: (1) precondition gate green (all 52 stay unresolved deterministically); (2) at the chosen accept, overall precision>=0.90 AND recall>=0.60 (we accept lower recall to protect precision; the deterministic cascade already covers the head, this tier is long-tail insurance); (3) per-language precision>=0.90 for en and vi; (4) ZERO semantic_negative auto-accepted at the chosen accept (hard zero — a single non-IT/soft-fluff false-accept blocks shipping); (5) offline no-op contract verified (NODE_ENV=test path resolves nothing, throws nothing). If precision>=0.90 is unreachable anywhere in 0.60-0.90, do NOT ship auto-accept: run the tier in needs_review-only mode (every embed hit becomes a suggestion, never an auto-resolve) and re-evaluate after taxonomy/alias expansion.

AMENDMENT 2026-06-05 (post adversarial label review): (a) NOISE-MARGIN RULE — the chosen accept must clear the closest measured negative by >= 0.02 (re-embed drift measured at +/-0.003; a 0.0006-margin pick at 0.68 was rejected as one noise sample away from a production false-accept). (b) Tie-break refined: among qualifying t -> max recall, then max precision, then lowest t. (c) PROVENANCE: 23 misses adversarially judged (judge + refuter per proposal); 8 alias additions moved 7 rows to eval-mentions (deterministic tier owns them now); 2 labels broadened (dashboards +tableau, async-python +python); 3 proposals refuted and NOT applied. Dataset now 45 rows (25 hits / 20 negatives). (d) RESULT @ accept=0.72: precision 1.000 overall+en+vi, recall 0.480 (recall bar 0.60 NOT met — precision-first ship; review band [0.64,0.72) captures 6 of 13 misses as needs_review suggestions; 5 wrong-target misses are confirmed model limitations, judges kept their labels). (e) OPERATIONAL INVARIANT: any matrix growth (backfill insert > 0) purges skill_resolutions for the tuple — cached resolutions are only valid against the matrix they were computed on.
